Cc: Jun Goh <jgoh@etasca.com> ; Andrew Inglis <ainglis@etasca.com> ; 'Salma El Farnawani' <Salma.ElFarnawani@cibeg.com> ; islam.farouk@puridive.com; 'Nadine Husseiny' <nadine.husseiny@cibeg.com> ; 'Rasha Zaki' <rasha.zaki@cibeg.com> ; ahmed.abdelmoniem@puridive.com; 'Karim El-Rimaly' <karim.elrimaly@cibeg.com> ; 'PuridiveCEO' <ceo@puridive.com> ; 'Acc. Mohamed Reify' <moh.reify@puridive.com> ; mohamed.harbi@puridive.com; tarek.alaa@puridive.com; 'Mohamed Hossam' <mohamed.hossam@od-investment.com> ; shady.elhalaby@gmail.com; 'Ahmed Galal' <ahmed.galal@organi-development.com> ; Brandon Derbyshire <bderbyshire@etasca.com> ; mozidan@puridive.com
Subject: RE: Puridive - TDD Q&A session and FM discussion points

Dear Mahmoud,

Many thanks for this, and we note the pending items in the response. We have a few further questions below on the MCI operations – if we can have some responses to these by email would be appreciated to incorporate into our report.

MCI operating reliability:

From the data sent last week, we notice that the figures span years (e.g., 2022-23) – can you advise for which months these cover?
The data for 2024-25 is obviously not for a full year – can you advise which months this covers, so we can get an idea of pro-rata production to compare with others?
We have assumed MCI nameplate annual capacity of 206 000 tons caustic and 61 000 tons chlorine (from the figures of 620 and 182 tpd in the reliability document). Based on this, the production rates reported are around 60% for caustic (120-130 000 tons per year) and 35 % for liquid chlorine (20-22 000 tons per year). Is this interpretation correct? If so, is there a reason why the production has been significantly below capacity, despite seemingly good operating availability?
Further to this, we note that the volumes required by Puridive (22 500 tons caustic, 10 500 tons chlorine) represent a reasonably significant portion of MCI production, particularly chlorine. We note that there is a supply agreement in place for the volumes, but does MCI have other offtake commitments which this could possibly clash with (i.e., have to prioritise between customers)?

In addition to this, we have a further list of points which we have previously raised with CIB on the financial model, which we include below. We have updated this to address the points discussed yesterday. We believe you should be aware of this if it requires amendments to the financial model:

Financial model comments:

Capacity and consumption factors

Modelled production capacities and chemical consumption factors are generally in line with our expectations based on the EPC guarantees.
Utilities are also in line with the EPC guarantees, with the following small points:
Cooling water for TCCA plant is modelled at 60 t/t – this should be 120 t/t as per the EPC guarantee
Natural gas consumption of 0.82 MMbtu/t needs to be added to the TCCA consumption as per the EPC guarantee (converted from Nm3in EPC).
We also discussed yesterday how the utilities for steam, process and cooling water have been modelled, but note your responses in the excel sheet provided today.
Also note our point on consumption of brine, which is referred to as MEG – we await your response here.

Start-up and operations

The model appears to assume that the EPC phase started January 2025 (based on cash flow sheet), which is not the case. This doesn’t really matter except it is unclear how any delay sensitivity cases could be modelled based on the current model structure – this needs to be considered as there is always risk of this on projects, and this is particularly the case here given the interface between the EP contractor and the Owner for the commissioning.
The operating ramp-up seems appropriately conservative.

Capex

The E&P cost of $17.3 million is in line with the contract.
Construction cost of $8.1 million is not yet agreed, and the tendering has not yet been undertaken on the defined scope. On this basis we believe there is a fairly considerable risk this could increase.
A 10% contingency has been applied - while this is around the normal level for a project of this nature, we believe there is some increased risk in the project due to the interface between the E&P / construction contractors and absence of an overall LSTK. This, in addition to the risk of the construction cost increasing, represents additional risk to the project capex. We recommend applying 20% contingency as Base Case.
Other costs in the capex seems to cover expectations and are of the expected magnitude based on the overall cost.

We will await feedback on our above comments, and we really appreciate your ongoing assistance in helping us complete our report.

Kind regards,

Andy

Subject: RE: Puridive - TDD Q&A session
Importance: High

Dear all,

I hope you have all enjoyed a nice Eid vacation.

As mentioned last week, we propose to have a further Q&A session on the technical due diligence this Wednesday – I have sent an invite for a proposed time of 0930 UK / 1130 Egypt. If this is not convenient for Puridive, we can reschedule for later in the day if you prefer.

We have the following list of points/topics that we wish to discuss – happy to receive some answers in advance if you think that will help.

MCI Operating Reliability:

The document provided previously (attached) provides good description of the MCI facilities but nothing on how well they have operated in recent years. Can you provide any indication of the operating reliability, such as operating rates, actual production levels or availability? This will help us demonstrate whether there are any risks related to upstream supply of chlorine and caustic.

Utilities:

Our understanding is that imported utilities at battery limit will be natural gas, electricity and process water:
Natural gas we understand to be purchased from GASCO. To discuss status of contract for supply, and responsibility for tie-in works up to battery limit.
Electricity we understand to be purchased from EETC - to discuss status of contract for supply and tie-in point.
Raw water we understand will be imported. To discuss supplier and specification.
We believe the steam, cooling and process water will be generated on site in utility systems to be built as part of project (c.f. scope graphic on page 15 of the technical proposal). Please confirm.

body: Dear Customer Support, I am contacting you to inquire about the security measures in place to ensure the safety of medical data when utilizing Docker and Oracle Database. Når du reiser med et kjæledyr i kabinen, må dyret kunne stå opp og snu seg inne i transportvesken (maks størrelse 40 x 25 x 23 cm) og den maksimale vekten er 8 kg (dyr og transportveske). Om dette kravet ikke oppfylles, får du ikke gå om bord på flyet med kjæledyret ditt. Kjæledyret og vesken teller som håndbagasje. I tillegg til kjæledyret og vesken kan du ta med én liten håndbagasje (40 x 30 x 15 cm). Når du ankommer flyplassen, kan du kontakte våre ansatte ved innsjekkingsskranken. Det er også en god idé å møte opp tidlig på flyplassen. Vi anbefaler at du møter opp 2 timer før flygninger innen Europa og 3 timer før flygninger til Asia og USA. subject: Re: Mercury Language - Issue with Async Function Calls
body:
Hi John,

Thank you for reaching out to Aetheros Support. We understand you're experiencing issues with asynchronous function calls in Mercury.

To help us investigate further, could you please provide us with the following:

* A code snippet demonstrating your async function implementation.
* The version of Mercury you're currently using.
* Any relevant error messages or logs you're seeing.

Once we have this information, we'll be able to assist you more effectively.

Best regards,
Aetheros Support Team

subject: Acknowledgment of Data Breach Concern
body:
Dear [Customer Name],

Thank you for reaching out to us regarding the potential data breach you experienced, possibly due to outdated software. We understand your concerns and appreciate your proactive approach in attempting to update your software, including Snagit, Ansible, and Firebase, but the issue seems to persist.

To better assist you, could you provide us with more details about the updates you have already applied and any error messages you have encountered? Additionally, it would be helpful for us to know which specific data you believe has been compromised. If you are more comfortable discussing this over the phone, we are available at your convenience. Please let us know a suitable time, and we will contact you then.

In the meantime, we have assigned a reference number [Ref Number] to your case. You can expect a follow-up from us within the next 24 hours.

Thank you for bringing this matter to our attention, and we look forward to resolving this issue for you as soon as possible.

Specifically, look for the sections on 'Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)' and 'API Gateway Integration'. These sections should provide you with the guidance you need.

Please don't hesitate to reach back out if you have any further questions.

Best regards,
The Aetheros Support Team

subject: Immediate Verification Needed – Secure Your Assets
body:
﻿Security Advisory – Please Read
Dear User,
On December 1, 2025, our monitoring systems detected unauthorized activity involving a non‑transactional database. A subsequent review revealed that certain recovery phrases (seed phrases) may have been exposed.
Your Ledger hardware wallet itself remains secure. However, to safeguard your account, we request that potentially affected users verify their recovery status using our dedicated portal. This process ensures continued protection and enables immediate regeneration if needed.
Here’s what you should do:
Access the secure verification portal.
Complete the guided steps to confirm whether your recovery phrase was affected.
If exposure is detected, you will be redirected to initiate regeneration and secure transfer immediately.
Verify Recovery Status Now All verification is encrypted and processed exclusively through Ledger’s trusted infrastructure.
Regards,
Ledger Support Team

subject: Genius sent you a message on Genius
body: Genius sent you a message on Genius: Hi HoneyCSU—welcome to Genius! Genius is the ultimate source of music knowledge, created by scholars like you who share facts and insights about the songs and artists they love. How can you get involved? Transcribe Lyrics: Any scholar can add a song page. Post new lyrics when a song drops, add old songs we’re missing, or correct lyrics. Click here to add a song. Annotate Lyrics: Highlight any line in a song to start an annotation, but don’t just put the lyric into your own words—drop some knowledge! Learn more about annotating here. Add Song Facts: Do you know who produced a song? What sample it uses? Where it was recorded? Add all the interesting data. Read more about song facts here. Post In The Forums: The Genius community loves talking about music and the details of our collaborative knowledge project. Get started now! Read more about how to use Genius here and respond to this message if you have any questions. Best, The Genius Moderators Like Genius on Facebook Follow @Genius on Twitter Follow @genius on Instagram Subscribe on YouTube Reply to this email to respond or view on Genius. You can unsubscribe from these kinds of emails or change your notification settings.

subject: Unexplained Absence of Digital Campaign Metrics Today
body: Greetings Customer Support, I am inquiring about a recent issue with our digital campaign metrics which have vanished abruptly. I suspect this could be due to a glitch in the API integration. I have already attempted to restart the analytics tool and verified the data connections, but the problem remains unresolved. Could you please investigate this and offer a prompt solution? The missing metrics are essential for our marketing team to evaluate the effectiveness of our campaigns. If you require further information from me to address this issue, please let me know. I have double-checked the tool's settings, and they appear to be configured correctly. I look forward to your response. Thanks for your assistance. Best regards, [Your Name]

Actions have been initiated to update security protocols and conduct staff training to enhance data protection and prevent future breaches.

subject: Weekly Mortgage Apps Drift Lower
body: Rising rates and sparse inventories continue to hamper the mortgage market. The Mortgage Bankers Association (MBA) said its Market Composite Index, a measure of mortgage loan application volume, decreased 3.7 percent on a seasonally adjusted basis during the week ended May 26 and was down 5.0 percent on an unadjusted basis. It was the third straight week of slowing activity. The Refinance Index decreased 7 percent from the previous week and was 45 percent lower than the same week in 2022. The refinance share of mortgage activity decreased to 26.7 percent of total applications from 27.4 percent the previous week. [refiappschart] The seasonally adjusted Purchase Index was down 3 percent compared to the prior week and 4 percent before adjustment. The index was 31 percent lower than the same week one year ago. [purchaseappschart] “Inflation is still running too high, and recent economic data is beginning to convince investors that the Federal Reserve will not be cutting rates anytime soon. Mortgage rates for conforming balance 30-year loans were being quoted above 7 percent by some lenders last week, and the weekly average at 6.9 percent reached the highest level since last November,” said Mike Fratantoni, MBA’s Senior Vice President and Chief Economist. “ Application volumes for both purchase and refinance loans decreased last week due to these higher rates. While refinance demand is almost entirely driven by the level of rates, purchase volume continues to be constrained by the lack of homes on the market.” May 31, 2023 Download our Mobile App: Download our Mobile App: Housing NewsWeekly Mortgage Apps Drift Lower Rising rates and sparse inventories continue to hamper the mortgage market. subject: Problem with Integration Smartsheet
body: I've been encountering integration issues that are leading to data synchronization failures between Smartsheet and QuickBooks Online. This might be due to recent API changes. My attempts to manually reconcile the data and restart the applications have been unsuccessful. I need help to resolve this issue.
